gmd:name.gco:CharacterString: NOAA Office for Coastal Management Website

gmd:description.gco:CharacterString: NOAA Office for Coastal Management Home Page

gmd:metadataStandardName.gco:CharacterString: ISO 19115-2 Geographic Information - Metadata Part 2 Extensions for imagery and gridded data

gmd:title.gco:CharacterString: 2006 US Army Corps of Engineers (USACE) East Coast Topo/Bathy Mapping Project for Florida, Georgia and South Carolina

gmd:description.gco:CharacterString: View the complete metadata record on InPort for more information about this dataset.

gmd:abstract.gco:CharacterString:
These data were collected by the Joint Airborne Lidar Bathymetry Technical Center of Expertise using the CHARTS system. The data includes hydrographic and topographic data. The data were collected in Dec 2005-Feb 2006 for the coasts of South Carolina, Georgia and the Atlantic coast of Florida.
Original contact information: Contact Org: NOAA Office for Coastal Management Phone: 843-740-1202 Email: coastal.info@noaa.gov This data set has 3639.0 columns and 3188.0.The data set was generated from a larger data set and includes all valid data within the requested geographic bounds.

gmd:purpose.gco:CharacterString: These data were collected as a part of the National Coastal Mapping Program to depict the elevations above and below water along the east coastal zone.

gmd:credit.gco:CharacterString: Acknowledgement of the Joint Airborne Lidar Bathymetry Center of Expertise (JALBTCX) would be appreciated in any publications or derived products.

gmd:name.gco:CharacterString: NOAA Office for Coastal Management Website

gmd:description.gco:CharacterString: NOAA Office for Coastal Management Home Page

gmd:name.gco:CharacterString: NOAA Office for Coastal Management Website

gmd:description.gco:CharacterString: NOAA Office for Coastal Management Home Page

gmd:otherConstraints.gco:CharacterString: This data set was compiled dynamically. It will be removed 10 days after 2026-01-30 from the NOAA system. Cite As: OCM Partners, [Date of Access]: 2006 US Army Corps of Engineers (USACE) East Coast Topo/Bathy Mapping Project for Florida, Georgia and South Carolina [Data Date Range], https://www.fisheries.noaa.gov/inport/item/50057.

gmd:useLimitation.gco:CharacterString: NOAA provides no warranty, nor accepts any liability occurring from any incomplete, incorrect, or misleading data, or from any incorrect, incomplete, or misleading use of the data. It is the responsibility of the user to determine whether or not the data is suitable for the intended purpose.

gmd:otherConstraints.gco:CharacterString: This data set was compiled dynamically. It will be removed 10 days after 2026-01-30 from the NOAA system. Access Constraints: None

This data set was compiled dynamically. It will be removed 10 days after 2026-01-30 from the NOAA system. Use Constraints: These data depict the heights at the time of the survey and are only accurate for that time. Users should be aware that temporal changes may have occurred since this data set was collected and some parts of this data may no longer represent actual surface conditions. Users should not use this data for critical applications without a full awareness of it's limitations.

This data set was compiled dynamically. It will be removed 10 days after 2026-01-30 from the NOAA system. Distribution Liability: Any conclusions drawn from analysis of this information are not the responsibility of NOAA, the Office for Coastal Management or it's partners.

gmd:description.gco:CharacterString: NOAA Data Management Plan for this record on InPort.

gmd:name.gco:CharacterString: NOAA Office for Coastal Management Website

gmd:description.gco:CharacterString: NOAA Office for Coastal Management Home Page
Create custom data files by choosing data area, product type, map projection, file format, datum, etc.

gmd:evaluationMethodDescription.gco:CharacterString: The data positions were obtained using post processed KGPS methods. The horizontal accuracy of the data is better than +/- 3.0m. ; Quantitative Value: 3.0 meters, Test that produced the value: 2 Sigma

The data positions were obtained using post processed KGPS methods. The vertical accuracy of the data is better than +/- 0.30m. ; Quantitative Value: 0.30 meters, Test that produced the value: 2 Sigma
These data covered the required survey area with minimal gaps in data coverage. The data postings are very consistent with postings at about 1.3m density.
Lidar data between different flight lines matched within 0.20 meters

gmd:description.gco:CharacterString: These data were collected using a SHOALS-1000T system. It is owned and operated by Fugro Pelagos performing contract survey services for the US Army Corps of Engineers. The system collects topographic lidar data at 10kHz and hydrographic data at 1kHz. The system also collects RGB imagery at 1Hz. Aircraft position, velocity and acceleration information are collected through a combination of Novatel and POS A/V equipment. Raw data are collected and transferred to the office for downloading and processing in SHOALS GCS software. GPS data are processed using POSPac software and the results are combined with the lidar data to produce 3-D positions for each lidar shot. These data are edited using Fledermaus software where anomalous data are removed from the dataset. The edited data are unloaded from SHOALS GCS, converted from ellipsoid to orthometric heights, based on the GEOID03 model, and split into greographic tiles covering approximately 5km each.

The NOAA Office for Coastal Management (OCM) received files in ascii format. The files contained lidar intensity and elevation measurements. OCM performed the following processing on the data to make it available within the Lidar Data Retrieval Tool (LDART): 1. Each hydro/topo last return ascii file was converted to las files. Default values for which there is no corresponding input were assigned as follows for each las elevation data record: intensity = 0, retn = 1, nret = 1, class = 0 2. The las files were sorted by latitude and the las header fields were updated.
For data managment purposes, the Office for Coastal Management converted the data from NAVD88 elevations to ellipsoid elevations using Geoid 03.
Source Contribution: This is an xyz dataset collected and provided by CESAM-OP-J, JALBTCX. | Source Geospatial Form: globe | Type of Source Media: disc
The dataset has been mosaiced, reprojected, and clipped as needed from the original input files using PDAL and GDAL commands.
